topic: Lovable vs Bolt.new Comparison 2026: Which AI App Builder Is Right for You?

key_points: Lovable is better for beginners with chat-first interface and structured planning stages
Bolt.new is better for developers who want full file tree, terminal, and code editor access
Both Pro plans cost $25/month but differ in usage models (credits vs tokens)
Lovable generated working prototype in 47 min, Bolt.new in 52 min benchmark test
Bolt.new's 'diffs' feature makes it faster by only updating changed code
Lovable consistently produces cleaner UI output — colors, spacing, layout well-structured
Bolt offers multi-model support (Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini) for different task types
Lovable's one-click deployment gives immediate sharable URL without extra configuration
Lovable handles the entire stack: frontend, backend, database, auth, file storage, hosting
Bolt.new can generate backend code (Express, API routes) but requires manual wiring of services
Complex SaaS apps with 20+ pages burn Bolt tokens faster than Lovable credits due to full codebase context processing
Bolt Cloud has been closing the gap with Lovable's Supabase integration in 2026
Bolt v2 introduced agentic capabilities: plans, iterates, and fixes issues autonomously
Bolt 2026 upgrades: Team Templates, Editable Netlify URLs, Opus 4.6 model, Figma import, AI image editing
Lovable Cloud provisions Supabase backend automatically with every workspace
Lovable's Business plan at $50/month adds SSO, team workspace, and security center
Both Lovable and Bolt.new are dominant AI app builders in 2026
Lovable excels at non-technical builders shipping real software
Bolt.new is a cloud IDE for developers who want faster workflows
v0 produces cleanest React code but is more frontend-focused than full-stack

stats.fact: Lovable hit $400M ARR in February 2026, up from $300M in January and $100M in July 2025
Lovable has approximately 8 million users and 146 employees as of early 2026
Lovable raised $330M Series B at a $6.6B valuation in December 2025 led by CapitalG and Menlo Ventures
Lovable generates $2.77M ARR per employee — exceptional revenue-per-employee efficiency
Lovable surpassed 25 million total projects and 100,000+ new projects per day at Series B time
Bolt.new achieved $40M ARR in 5 months, making it the second-fastest-growing software product after ChatGPT
Bolt.new has 5 million registered users and was adding 1M+ users per month as of 2025
Bolt.new valued at ~$700M after $105.5M Series B led by Emergence Capital and GV in January 2025
Bolt.new receives ~5.43 million monthly visits as of March 2026 (Semrush data)
Bolt.new's biggest markets: India (20.66%), USA (16.15%), Brazil (7.18%)
Lovable free plan: 5 daily credits (30/month max); Pro $25/month: 100 credits + 5 daily
Bolt.new free plan: 1M tokens/month; Pro $25/month: 10M tokens with rollover

angles: Revenue gap tells a story: Lovable's $400M ARR vs Bolt's $40M ARR shows product-first wins in the no-code market
The 'vibe coding' revolution — both tools let non-developers ship real products without writing code
Backend convergence in 2026: Bolt Cloud has closed much of the gap with Lovable's native Supabase integration
Credits vs tokens: Lovable's predictable credit model vs Bolt's token model that scales with project complexity
Lovable for founders, Bolt for developers — the tools serve different mental models about what 'building' means
Prototype speed test: Lovable at 47 minutes vs Bolt.new at 52 minutes for comparable full-stack app
Enterprise readiness: Lovable's $50/month Business tier with SSO vs Bolt's Teams plan at $30/member/month
The agentic shift: Bolt v2 now autonomously plans, iterates, and fixes before you ask
Multi-model advantage: Bolt's choice between Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini gives developers task-specific optimization
keywords: bolt new pricing vs lovable pricing
outline: H2: Lovable vs Bolt.new at a Glance: 2026 Overview
H2: Who Should Use Each Tool? Target Audience Breakdown
H2: Pricing Breakdown: Credits vs Tokens
H2: Backend Capabilities: Supabase vs Bolt Cloud
H2: UI Quality and Code Output: Side-by-Side Results
H2: Speed and Performance: Prototype Benchmark
H2: Verdict: Which AI App Builder Is Right for You in 2026?
